GAM demo for partial dependence plots

Author

Kendra Wyant

Published

June 2, 2026

Set Up Environment

library(tidyverse)
── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.1     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(mgcv)
Loading required package: nlme

Attaching package: 'nlme'

The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':

    collapse

This is mgcv 1.9-4. For overview type '?mgcv'.
library(patchwork)

devtools::source_url("https://github.com/jjcurtin/lab_support/blob/main/format_path.R?raw=true")
ℹ SHA-1 hash of file is "0faa14c0c44c2635216370888b7da9bfa8d07979"
devtools::source_url("https://github.com/jjcurtin/lab_support/blob/main/chtc/static_files/fun_chtc.R?raw=true")
ℹ SHA-1 hash of file is "2f3d9db3468bfcbb5b05b98deb6858c6bb04b871"
path_models <- format_path(str_c("risk2/models/combined"))

Data

shaps <- read_rds(here::here(path_models, 
                             "shapsgrp_1_x_5_day_v10_kfold_full_final.rds"))

Get top 20 global shaps across sample

(shaps_10 <- shaps |> 
  group_by(variable_grp) |>
  summarize(mean_value = mean(abs(value)), .groups = "drop") |> 
  arrange(desc(mean_value)) |> 
  slice_head(n = 20) |> 
   pull(variable_grp))
 [1] "Past opioid use, Diff (EMA)"       "Urge, Raw (EMA)"                  
 [3] "Abstinence confidence, Raw (EMA)"  "Stimulant use, Diff (EMA)"        
 [5] "Pleasant event, Diff (EMA)"        "Abstinence motivation, Raw (EMA)" 
 [7] "Helpful locations, Diff (gps)"     "Religious, Diff (gps)"            
 [9] "Location variance, Diff (gps)"     "Harmful locations, Diff (gps)"    
[11] "Risky situation, Diff (EMA)"       "Sedative use, Diff (EMA)"         
[13] "Helpful locations, Raw (gps)"      "Urge, Diff (EMA)"                 
[15] "Harmful locations, Raw (gps)"      "Abstinence confidence, Diff (EMA)"
[17] "Age (demographic)"                 "Geography (demographic)"          
[19] "Angry, Diff (EMA)"                 "Pleasant event, Raw (EMA)"

Past Opioid Use

Original Plot

shaps |>
  filter(variable_grp == "Past opioid use, Diff (EMA)") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = rfvalue, y = value)) +
  geom_point(alpha = .3) +
  facet_wrap(~ variable_grp, scales = "free", ncol = 4) +
  labs(
    title = NULL,
    x = "z-score raw feature score",
    y = "Shapley value"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )

Fit GAM

Source - https://asbates.rbind.io/2019/03/15/generalized-additive-models-part-2-mgcv/

df <- shaps |>
  filter(variable_grp == "Past opioid use, Diff (EMA)")

past_use <- gam(value ~ s(rfvalue, k = 300),
                    data = df,
                    method = "REML")

plot(past_use)

Check convergence and dimension of smoothing

Ideally:

  • edf should not equal k
  • p-value should not be significant
  • k-index shoiuld be close to 1

Default k is 10 - increase if above thresholds are not met

gam.check(past_use)

Method: REML   Optimizer: outer newton
full convergence after 4 iterations.
Gradient range [-1.354967e-05,7.261524e-06]
(score -1550.042 & scale 0.05536379).
Hessian positive definite, eigenvalue range [106.7018,41076.95].
Model rank =  300 / 300 

Basis dimension (k) checking results. Low p-value (k-index<1) may
indicate that k is too low, especially if edf is close to k'.

            k' edf k-index p-value
s(rfvalue) 299 275    0.99    0.15

Add GAM to Plot

Get predictions

preds <- tibble(
  rfvalue = seq(
    min(df$rfvalue, na.rm = TRUE),
    max(df$rfvalue, na.rm = TRUE),
    length.out = 100
  )
)

preds$fit <- predict(past_use, newdata = preds)

Add to plot

(plot_1 <- df |>
  ggplot(aes(x = rfvalue, y = value)) +
  geom_point(alpha = .3) +
  geom_line(data = preds, aes(x = rfvalue, y = fit), linewidth = 1, color = "blue") +
  facet_wrap(~ variable_grp, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = NULL, x = "z-score raw feature score", y = "Shapley value") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")))

Urge

df <- shaps |>
  filter(variable_grp == "Urge, Raw (EMA)")

urge <- gam(value ~ s(rfvalue, k = 10),
                    data = df,
                    method = "REML")

plot(urge)
gam.check(urge)

Method: REML   Optimizer: outer newton
full convergence after 8 iterations.
Gradient range [-0.004844447,0.00481599]
(score -19874.42 & scale 0.03605491).
Hessian positive definite, eigenvalue range [3.987425,41077.01].
Model rank =  10 / 10 

Basis dimension (k) checking results. Low p-value (k-index<1) may
indicate that k is too low, especially if edf is close to k'.

             k'  edf k-index p-value    
s(rfvalue) 9.00 8.98    0.17  <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
preds <- tibble(
  rfvalue = seq(
    min(df$rfvalue, na.rm = TRUE),
    max(df$rfvalue, na.rm = TRUE),
    length.out = 100
  )
)

preds$fit <- predict(urge, newdata = preds)

(plot_2 <- df |>
  ggplot(aes(x = rfvalue, y = value)) +
  geom_point(alpha = .3) +
  geom_line(data = preds, aes(x = rfvalue, y = fit), linewidth = 1, color = "blue") +
  facet_wrap(~ variable_grp, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = NULL, x = "z-score raw feature score", y = "Shapley value") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")))
urge <- gam(value ~ s(rfvalue, k = 300),
                    data = df,
                    method = "REML")

plot(urge)
gam.check(urge)

Method: REML   Optimizer: outer newton
full convergence after 5 iterations.
Gradient range [-0.04573104,0.04036448]
(score -64895.49 & scale 0.01173617).
Hessian positive definite, eigenvalue range [138.0796,41076.57].
Model rank =  300 / 300 

Basis dimension (k) checking results. Low p-value (k-index<1) may
indicate that k is too low, especially if edf is close to k'.

            k' edf k-index p-value    
s(rfvalue) 299 296     0.5  <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
preds <- tibble(
  rfvalue = seq(
    min(df$rfvalue, na.rm = TRUE),
    max(df$rfvalue, na.rm = TRUE),
    length.out = 100
  )
)

preds$fit <- predict(urge, newdata = preds)

(plot_2 <- df |>
  ggplot(aes(x = rfvalue, y = value)) +
  geom_point(alpha = .3) +
  geom_line(data = preds, aes(x = rfvalue, y = fit), linewidth = 1, color = "blue") +
  facet_wrap(~ variable_grp, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = NULL, x = "z-score raw feature score", y = "Shapley value") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")))
